Motion To Compel Compliance With Civil Subpoena (Duces Tecum) To Michael Ferguson And For Monetary Sanctions
Off Calendar Sep-15-2023 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearance by any party or counsel.

Ruling on: PLAINTIFFS LARRY ROSENBERGER, DIANE ROSENBERGER'S Motion To Compel Compliance With Civil Subpoena (Duces Tecum) To Michael Ferguson And For Monetary Sanctions.

There being no appearances, the tentative ruling is adopted as follows:

The motion is GRANTED IN PART. Defendant does not dispute his failure to serve code-complaint responses to the subpoena. Defendant shall do so by September 29, 2023. As to requests for production numbers 2, 3, 8, 9, 13, 21-23, 25, 27, 28, 34, and 61-64, the motion is GRANTED. Defendant asserts he has produced all documents "in his possession" and "is not required to produce documents he has no access to or to produce business records on separate entities." Defendant provides no authority for these assertions. Under California law, Defendant must produce all responsive documents in his possession, custody, or control, which includes all documents he has the legal right to obtain. Further, the obligation to produce documents is not limited to documents that existed at an earlier date, as Defendant asserts, but applies to documents in existence as of the date of this order. Defendant shall produce all non-privileged documents in his possession, custody, or control by September 29, 2023 or such later date as the parties may agree. The parties' requests for sanctions are DENIED. No party has acted without substantial justification.

Judge Pro Tem Kahn to sign the proposed order.

Judge Pro Tem: Matthew Kahn; Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda; Not Reported. (302/JPT)

HEARING RE: PETITIONERS LARRY ROSENBERGER and DIANE ROSENBERGER's Motion for Issuance of OSC Re Contempt; Imposition Of Personal Liability; Additional Restraining Orders.

Matter argued and submitted. The Court adopts its tentative ruling with a modification as to the date of the OSC Re Contempt hearing, as follows:

Plaintiff's request for an OSC re contempt is granted. Plaintiff presents facts indicating: the court's jurisdiction; Defendant's knowledge of the order disobeyed; Defendant's ability to comply; and Defendant's willful disobedience of the order. (See Koehler v. Superior Court (2010) 181 Cal.App.4th 1153.)

Plaintiff shall prepare an OSC re contempt and serve it on defendants by May 16, 2022. (See In re M.R. (2013) 220 Cal.App.4th 49, 63, fn.3 [service of OSC re contempt].) The hearing will occur on June 24, 2022 at 1:30 p.m. in department 302 and plaintiff shall provide a court reporter. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. The reporter may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. The day prior to the hearing please provide the following: the reporter's name, CSR and telephone numbers, and the reporter's individual work email address. The OSC shall also set forth the distinct counts of contempt.

Plaintiff's requests for other relief will be addressed after the contempt hearing.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr.; Clerk: Sean Kane; Not Reported (302/RBU)
